mission.title: kanister: [BUG] Problem with long running phase
mission.description: [BUG] Problem with long running phase. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kanister troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kanister deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kanister -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kanister
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kanister -n kanister --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kanister configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kanister configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kanister -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kanister
kubectl get configmap -n kanister -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kanister
```
When phase takes about 6 hours, the operator (0.81.0) does not set the `snapshot_id` in the `ActionSet` despite using kando for this purpose.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG] Problem with long running phase
steps.description:
We have started working on the issue, but unfortunately we don't have the resolution yet. I will keep updating the progress here.
```yaml
backup:
    kind: Deployment
    outputArtifacts:
      mysql_backup:
        keyValue:
          snapshot_id: '{{ .Phases.backupMySQL.Output.snapshot_id }}'
    phases:
      - args:
          command:
            - bash
            - '-o'
            - errexit
            - '-o'
            - pipefail
            - '-o'
            - errtrace
            - '-c'
            - |
              sleep 20000
              kando output snapshot_id 6c1b0940
          namespace: mysql
        func: KubeTask
        name: backupMySQL
```

steps.title: Confirm [BUG] Problem with long running phase is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kanister -n kanister --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kanister --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: We have started working on the issue, but unfortunately we don't have the resolution yet. I will keep updating the progress here.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kanister installed or the issue environment reproducible.
